question_frequency.rationale: Only one didactic question is posed (1D vs 2D array choice). Other potentially teachable moments — base cases, why the recurrence equals C(i+j,i), traversal direction subtlety — are explained rather than asked.

question_quality.rationale: The single question targets a genuinely meaningful trade-off (space optimization vs clarity) and surfaces the subtle left-to-right traversal dependency in the 1D rolling array, which is a classic DP gotcha.

scaffolding.rationale: The two options are well-framed and the follow-up reinforces the traversal-direction subtlety. But with just one question, there's no real progression — and the closed form is given upfront rather than being elicited.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and collegial; the Pair acknowledges the user's reasoning, refines its own framing ("My lean toward A was about ingraining the space-optimization reflex early..."), then proceeds. Conversation feels natural.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is correct and produces 137,846,528,820, with assertion confirming DP matches C(40,20). Comparable to baseline, which made an arithmetic typo in prose but had correct logic.

friction.rationale: 2 turns / 79s vs baseline's 1 turn / 53s — modestly longer with proportional pedagogical payoff (the 1D-vs-2D fork plus actual code execution and verification). Reasonable trade.
